query: SELECT id, code, title, description FROM courses ORDER BY id LIMIT 10;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute SELECT id, code, title, description FROM courses ORDER BY id LIMIT 10;

rows.title: Systematic Theology
rows.description: This foundational course offers a comprehensive study of Christian doctrine organized by major theological loci including theology proper, Christology, pneumatology, anthropology, hamartiology, soteriology, ecclesiology, and eschatology. Students will engage with classical theological formulations, contemporary debates, and biblical foundations to develop a coherent and unified system of belief. The course emphasizes how systematic theology informs faithful Christian practice, shapes pastoral ministry, and equips believers to articulate and defend essential doctrines with clarity, precision, and biblical fidelity.

rows.title: Biblical Hermeneutics
rows.description: This essential course trains students in the art and science of biblical interpretation, equipping them with sound principles for understanding Scripture in its original context and applying it faithfully today. Students will master grammatical-historical interpretation, genre analysis, literary criticism, cultural background study, and responsible application methods. The course addresses common interpretive errors, explores the role of the Holy Spirit in illumination, and provides practical tools for exegesis that honor the text's intended meaning while bridging the gap between ancient text and contemporary application.

rows.title: Fundamentals of Apologetics
rows.description: This intellectually rigorous course equips students to defend the Christian faith through reasoned arguments, historical evidence, and philosophical inquiry. The curriculum addresses classical proofs for God's existence, the problem of evil, the reliability and authority of Scripture, the historical evidence for the resurrection, and responses to contemporary objections from atheism, skepticism, and alternative worldviews. Students will develop confidence in engaging critics graciously while providing compelling reasons for Christian belief, preparing them to give an answer for the hope within them with gentleness and respect.

rows.title: Evangelism and Discipleship
rows.description: This practical theology course explores biblical foundations and effective strategies for sharing the gospel and making disciples. Students will examine Jesus' Great Commission, apostolic evangelistic methods, and contemporary approaches to personal and mass evangelism. The course covers gospel presentation, cultural sensitivity, relational evangelism, and the process of spiritual multiplication. Special emphasis is placed on developing sustainable discipleship systems that move new believers from conversion through spiritual maturity, equipping them to reproduce their faith in others and fulfill the mission of the church.

rows.title: Discipleship Training
rows.description: This transformative course provides a comprehensive framework for spiritual formation and leadership development within the body of Christ. Students will explore biblical models of discipleship, mentoring relationships, spiritual growth stages, and practical methods for guiding believers toward Christlikeness. The curriculum addresses character development, spiritual disciplines, accountability structures, and multiplication principles that create self-sustaining discipleship cultures. Graduates will be equipped to design and implement effective discipleship pathways that produce mature, reproducing disciples who impact their communities for the kingdom of God.

rows.title: Prayer and Intercession
rows.description: This spiritually formative course delves into the theology and practice of prayer as the foundation of Christian life and ministry. Students will study biblical teachings on prayer, explore various prayer models and traditions, and develop a robust personal prayer life. The course examines intercessory prayer, spiritual warfare, corporate prayer, contemplative practices, and the role of prayer in revival and spiritual awakening. Through theological reflection and practical application, students will cultivate deeper intimacy with God and learn to mobilize prayer movements that release divine power for personal transformation, church renewal, and kingdom advancement.

rows.title: Leadership
rows.description: This comprehensive course examines biblical principles and contemporary best practices for effective Christian leadership in church and paracurch contexts. Students will explore servant leadership models, vision casting, team building, organizational development, change management, and conflict resolution. The curriculum integrates theological foundations with practical leadership skills, addressing ethical decision-making, cultural intelligence, and adaptive leadership in rapidly changing ministry environments. Graduates will be equipped to lead with integrity, wisdom, and spiritual authority while developing leadership pipelines that multiply influence and advance God's kingdom purposes.

rows.title: Pastoral Counseling
rows.description: This integrative course prepares students for the pastoral care and counseling dimensions of ministry by combining biblical wisdom, theological reflection, and counseling theory. Students will explore the pastor's role as shepherd and counselor, addressing common issues such as grief, trauma, marriage and family conflicts, addiction, mental health, and spiritual crises. The curriculum emphasizes biblical counseling approaches while incorporating insights from psychology and therapeutic best practices. Graduates will be equipped to provide compassionate, Christ-centered care that promotes healing, restoration, and spiritual growth within appropriate ethical and professional boundaries.

rows.title: Church Administration
rows.description: This practical course equips ministry leaders with essential skills for effective church management and organizational leadership. Students will study governance structures, financial stewardship, legal compliance, staff management, volunteer mobilization, facilities management, and strategic planning. The curriculum balances theological foundations with administrative best practices, addressing the unique challenges of leading nonprofit religious organizations. Special attention is given to developing systems that support the church's mission while maintaining accountability, sustainability, and kingdom-focused priorities. Graduates will be prepared to administrate church operations with excellence, integrity, and missional effectiveness.

rows.title: Homiletics
rows.description: This essential course trains students in the art and craft of biblical preaching, equipping them to communicate God's Word with clarity, power, and relevance. Students will master sermon preparation methods, exegetical techniques, rhetorical strategies, and delivery skills. The curriculum covers various sermon forms including expository, topical, and narrative preaching, while addressing contemporary challenges such as cultural engagement, application, and preaching to diverse audiences. Through theological reflection, practical exercises, and constructive feedback, students will develop their unique preaching voice and learn to proclaim Scripture faithfully in ways that transform lives and build up the body of Christ.
